texts.label: text
texts.orig: Carbon dioxide (CO2) is the most important GHG (greenhouse gas) and its annual emissions grew by 80% between 1970 and 2004 though the long-term trend of declining CO2 emissions per unit of energy supplied reversed after 2000. CO2 from fossil fuel uses attributes to almost 60% of all different anthropogenic GHGs (CO2, CH4, N2O, etc.) [1]. Combustion of fossil fuels in natural gas fired and coal fired power plants make up the largest source of CO2 emissions in Europe [2].
texts.text: Carbon dioxide (CO2) is the most important GHG (greenhouse gas) and its annual emissions grew by 80% between 1970 and 2004 though the long-term trend of declining CO2 emissions per unit of energy supplied reversed after 2000. CO2 from fossil fuel uses attributes to almost 60% of all different anthropogenic GHGs (CO2, CH4, N2O, etc.) [1]. Combustion of fossil fuels in natural gas fired and coal fired power plants make up the largest source of CO2 emissions in Europe [2].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The conventional process for CO2 capture is by reversible solvent absorption using amines [3]. In general, this process achieves a high level of CO2 capture but it requires a lot of energy and make-up of amines due to degradation over time. The impact of energy cost in conventional solvent absorption processes points to a need for development of more efficient and environmentally friendly processes for CO2 capture [4].
texts.text: The conventional process for CO2 capture is by reversible solvent absorption using amines [3]. In general, this process achieves a high level of CO2 capture but it requires a lot of energy and make-up of amines due to degradation over time. The impact of energy cost in conventional solvent absorption processes points to a need for development of more efficient and environmentally friendly processes for CO2 capture [4].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Polyvinyamine (PVAm) Fixed-Site-Carrier (FSC) composite membrane has been reported to have a good potential to be applied for large scale for CO2 capture applications with respect to high permeance and high selectivity. In this study, the membrane was investigated with respect to pH of the casting solution as a step for optimization of the membrane. It was found out that the pH of the PVAm casting solution could be easily controlled with a simple agent. The degree of protonation of the functional groups depends on the pH of the casting solution and influenced the reaction of carriers with CO2 and the solubility of CO2 in the FSC membrane swollen by water vapor in the feed flue gas. So it was expected that the pH could affect greatly the membrane's CO2 capture performance. Membranes from casting solutions of different pH have been prepared, characterized and tested in a permeation set-up for CO2 separation from humidified flue gas. Permeation tests have shown that the performance of the PVAm FSC membrane for CO2 capture could be enhanced remarkably by pH control. This is explained by the increased number of free amine groups available for facilitated transport of CO2 with increasing pH. At low pH, it is assumed that the decreased number of free amines and the increased viscosity of casting solution have resulted in the decreased membrane performance.
texts.text: Polyvinyamine (PVAm) Fixed-Site-Carrier (FSC) composite membrane has been reported to have a good potential to be applied for large scale for CO2 capture applications with respect to high permeance and high selectivity. In this study, the membrane was investigated with respect to pH of the casting solution as a step for optimization of the membrane. It was found out that the pH of the PVAm casting solution could be easily controlled with a simple agent. The degree of protonation of the functional groups depends on the pH of the casting solution and influenced the reaction of carriers with CO2 and the solubility of CO2 in the FSC membrane swollen by water vapor in the feed flue gas. So it was expected that the pH could affect greatly the membrane's CO2 capture performance. Membranes from casting solutions of different pH have been prepared, characterized and tested in a permeation set-up for CO2 separation from humidified flue gas. Permeation tests have shown that the performance of the PVAm FSC membrane for CO2 capture could be enhanced remarkably by pH control. This is explained by the increased number of free amine groups available for facilitated transport of CO2 with increasing pH. At low pH, it is assumed that the decreased number of free amines and the increased viscosity of casting solution have resulted in the decreased membrane performance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: durable for long-term continuous operation and withstand contamination by SO2, NOx, fly ash, etc. Furthermore they need to be cost effective for module manufacturing. To make the membrane CO2 capture process competitive with the solvent absorption process, membranes need to have good selectivity and high CO2 permeability. However there is a trade-off between permeability and selectivity for most polymeric membranes which is known as ''the Robeson upper-bound'' [14]. The revised upper bound [15] shows an enhanced bound due to the large improvements in membrane performance since its first upper bound in the 90 0 s.
texts.text: durable for long-term continuous operation and withstand contamination by SO2, NOx, fly ash, etc. Furthermore they need to be cost effective for module manufacturing. To make the membrane CO2 capture process competitive with the solvent absorption process, membranes need to have good selectivity and high CO2 permeability. However there is a trade-off between permeability and selectivity for most polymeric membranes which is known as ''the Robeson upper-bound'' [14]. The revised upper bound [15] shows an enhanced bound due to the large improvements in membrane performance since its first upper bound in the 90 0 s.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The composition of flue gas can vary significantly depending on the source of post combustion process. Natural gas fired power plants have about 4-10% CO2 in the exhaust flue gas, and simulations presented in [12] is based on 4% CO2, 13% O2, 76% N2 and 7% H2O at 1 bar. Coal fired power plants on the other hand have a higher CO2 concentration and therefore a larger driving force for CO2 transport through the membrane. A typical flue gas from a coal fired plant may consist of 13.5% CO2, 70.1% N2, 3.7% O2, 0.8% Ar and 11.9% H2O at 1 bar [16]. In general CO2 in flue gas is so much diluted with nitrogen that it does not induce enough driving force for separation by membranes with low selectivity and permeability. In the majority of industrial processes where CO2 capture can occur, the feed stream is saturated with water. Upon exposure to the water, the permeability of CO2 may reduce

Coal fired power plants on the other hand have a higher CO2 concentration and therefore a larger driving force for CO2 transport through the membrane. A typical flue gas from a coal fired plant may consist of 13.5% CO2, 70.1% N2, 3.7% O2, 0.8% Ar and 11.9% H2O at 1 bar [16]. In general CO2 in flue gas is so much diluted with nitrogen that it does not induce enough driving force for separation by membranes with low selectivity and permeability. texts.label: text
texts.orig: due to competitive sorption of water in membrane [17]. Researchers seem to agree on that in order for membranes to be a viable economical solution for CO2 capture from flue gas, the separation needs to be enhanced both with respect to permeability and selectivity. This may be done either by a facilitated transport membrane or by a smart process design.
texts.text: due to competitive sorption of water in membrane [17]. Researchers seem to agree on that in order for membranes to be a viable economical solution for CO2 capture from flue gas, the separation needs to be enhanced both with respect to permeability and selectivity. This may be done either by a facilitated transport membrane or by a smart process design.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The water vapor in the flue gas humidifies the FSC membrane, hence increasing the mobility of the carrier and permeating gas molecules. The water in the humidified FSC membrane also enhances the transport of CO2 by participating in the reversible reaction of CO2 with the carrier which is usually an amino group. Therefore a fixed-site-carrier (FSC) membrane can be an excellent choice for CO2 separation from water vapor saturated diluted gases such as flue gas [6,8,10-13]. Facilitated transport membranes can be highly selective, especially at low partial pressure driving forces. Very high permeability compared to conventional membranes can be obtained for the component of interest at very low concentration driving forces [18].
texts.text: The water vapor in the flue gas humidifies the FSC membrane, hence increasing the mobility of the carrier and permeating gas molecules. The water in the humidified FSC membrane also enhances the transport of CO2 by participating in the reversible reaction of CO2 with the carrier which is usually an amino group. Therefore a fixed-site-carrier (FSC) membrane can be an excellent choice for CO2 separation from water vapor saturated diluted gases such as flue gas [6,8,10-13]. Facilitated transport membranes can be highly selective, especially at low partial pressure driving forces. Very high permeability compared to conventional membranes can be obtained for the component of interest at very low concentration driving forces [18].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since the first polyvinylamine (PVAm) FSC membrane, with excellent CO2/CH4 separation properties, was reported by our group in 2004 [19] the FSC membranes have been extensively and intensively investigated by MEMFO (membrane research group at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Trondheim, Norway) [10-13,19-24]. The first study [19] focused on material properties and synthesis. PVAm was treated with various cross-linking agents to improve its properties and the effect of molecular weight in the selective PVAm layer and the support material was investigated. The influence of molecular weight of PVAm on CO2 separation from CO2/N2 mixture gas was further investigated by water uptake and gas sorption studies [20]. The effect of process parameters like the pressure on the feed and permeate side and the use of sweep gas or vacuum were also intensively studied [21]. Mechanically reinforced PVAm-PVA blend membranes were studied for high pressure CO2/CH4 separation applications [22,23].
texts.text: Since the first polyvinylamine (PVAm) FSC membrane, with excellent CO2/CH4 separation properties, was reported by our group in 2004 [19] the FSC membranes have been extensively and intensively investigated by MEMFO (membrane research group at the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Trondheim, Norway) [10-13,19-24]. The first study [19] focused on material properties and synthesis. PVAm was treated with various cross-linking agents to improve its properties and the effect of molecular weight in the selective PVAm layer and the support material was investigated. The influence of molecular weight of PVAm on CO2 separation from CO2/N2 mixture gas was further investigated by water uptake and gas sorption studies [20]. The effect of process parameters like the pressure on the feed and permeate side and the use of sweep gas or vacuum were also intensively studied [21]. Mechanically reinforced PVAm-PVA blend membranes were studied for high pressure CO2/CH4 separation applications [22,23].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Contamination by hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and hydrocarbons in the natural gas sweetening process and by acidic gases such as SO2 and NOx in flue gas purification are challenges that need to be addressed for membranes to be industrially viable. The effect of contaminants on PVAm FSC membrane performance has also been investigated in our lab. The PVAm FSC membranes have shown good resistance towards such contaminants - these results are in the progress of being published. The membranes have also been produced as flat sheets on pilot scale for tests at real flue gas exposure [24].
texts.text: Contamination by hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and hydrocarbons in the natural gas sweetening process and by acidic gases such as SO2 and NOx in flue gas purification are challenges that need to be addressed for membranes to be industrially viable. The effect of contaminants on PVAm FSC membrane performance has also been investigated in our lab. The PVAm FSC membranes have shown good resistance towards such contaminants - these results are in the progress of being published. The membranes have also been produced as flat sheets on pilot scale for tests at real flue gas exposure [24].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As already stated, post combustion flue gas with low CO2 concentration and pressure is usually considered to have too low driving force for large scale commercial application of membranes. Based on the results from experiments under various conditions [19-24], techno-economic analyses of PVAm FSC membranes have been performed for different scenarios, e.g. 2 membrane stages and recycles [11,12]. The analysis was carried out using an in-house membrane program interfaced with process simulation program (AspenHysis) to investigate the influence of different process parameters and membrane stage configurations on the energy demand and post combustion gas process cost [11]. The analysis compared a FSC membrane process for post combustion CO2 capture with comparable amine absorption process. Applications of the FSC membrane for IGCC (Integrated Gasification Combined Cycle) and steel making industry have shown good potential also through the techno-economic analysis [10,13].
texts.text: As already stated, post combustion flue gas with low CO2 concentration and pressure is usually considered to have too low driving force for large scale commercial application of membranes. Based on the results from experiments under various conditions [19-24], techno-economic analyses of PVAm FSC membranes have been performed for different scenarios, e.g. 2 membrane stages and recycles [11,12]. The analysis was carried out using an in-house membrane program interfaced with process simulation program (AspenHysis) to investigate the influence of different process parameters and membrane stage configurations on the energy demand and post combustion gas process cost [11]. The analysis compared a FSC membrane process for post combustion CO2 capture with comparable amine absorption process. Applications of the FSC membrane for IGCC (Integrated Gasification Combined Cycle) and steel making industry have shown good potential also through the techno-economic analysis [10,13].

texts.orig: Carrier-facilitated transport membrane incorporates a carrier in the membrane which will react reversibly with one specific component in the feed gas. The reversible reaction between the carrier and a component A increases the flux of that component across the membrane, which is described by Eq. (3).
texts.text: Carrier-facilitated transport membrane incorporates a carrier in the membrane which will react reversibly with one specific component in the feed gas. The reversible reaction between the carrier and a component A increases the flux of that component across the membrane, which is described by Eq. (3).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The permeance is the permeability divided by the membrane thickness, which makes it easier to compare composite membranes of different total thickness and swelling. The selectivity can also be estimated simply by dividing the permeance of a component with the permeance of the other component transported through the membrane.
texts.text: The permeance is the permeability divided by the membrane thickness, which makes it easier to compare composite membranes of different total thickness and swelling. The selectivity can also be estimated simply by dividing the permeance of a component with the permeance of the other component transported through the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As a consequence of the additional contribution to the separation from the carrier-facilitated transport as expressed by Eq. (4) second term, the facilitated transport membrane can be highly selective and permeable for a specific component. It was early suggested that the CO2 is transported as carbamate and bicarbonate and that the transport is promoted by water from the humid flue gas [26]. This theory has later been confirmed by many researchers investigating CO2 facilitated transport membranes, and is also adopted by the authors of the current paper.

(4) second term, the facilitated transport membrane can be highly selective and permeable for a specific component. It was early suggested that the CO2 is transported as carbamate and bicarbonate and that the transport is promoted by water from the humid flue gas [26]. This theory has later been confirmed by many researchers investigating CO2 facilitated transport membranes, and is also adopted by the authors of the current paper.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The facilitated transport of CO2 is illustrated schematically in Fig. 1: the facilitated transport increases the CO2 transport through the membrane, while the transport of non-reactive gases like nitrogen or methane is not affected by the facilitated transport. Thus, in the novel PVAm FSC membrane the presence of water is actually advantageous as it promotes the transport of CO2, and the swollen FSC membrane may have a CO2 diffusivity comparable to diffusivity of a liquid [25].
texts.text: The facilitated transport of CO2 is illustrated schematically in Fig. 1: the facilitated transport increases the CO2 transport through the membrane, while the transport of non-reactive gases like nitrogen or methane is not affected by the facilitated transport. Thus, in the novel PVAm FSC membrane the presence of water is actually advantageous as it promotes the transport of CO2, and the swollen FSC membrane may have a CO2 diffusivity comparable to diffusivity of a liquid [25].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pH of the PVAm casting solution determines the relative amounts of free amine groups and the corresponding ammonium salt. At low pH most of the amine groups are protonated and converted into less reactive ammonium groups. At high pH on the other hand, most amine groups are present as neutral amines and very few are present in the ammonium form. The charge density decreases almost linearly with increasing pH. According to Chen et al. and Badesso et al. [27,31], 80-90% of amine groups are protonated to NH3 þ at pH 4, about 65% are NH3 þ groups at pH 6 and about 45% are NH3 þ groups at pH 8 while the rest is free amine groups in PVAm water solution. Kobayashi et al. [28] also reported that all the amino groups attached to the polymer chain are protonated at pH 3 and pH greatly affects the physicalchemical properties of PVAm. The chemical structure of PVAm at different pH is illustrated in Fig. 2.
texts.text: The pH of the PVAm casting solution determines the relative amounts of free amine groups and the corresponding ammonium salt. At low pH most of the amine groups are protonated and converted into less reactive ammonium groups. At high pH on the other hand, most amine groups are present as neutral amines and very few are present in the ammonium form. The charge density decreases almost linearly with increasing pH. According to Chen et al. and Badesso et al. [27,31], 80-90% of amine groups are protonated to NH3 þ at pH 4, about 65% are NH3 þ groups at pH 6 and about 45% are NH3 þ groups at pH 8 while the rest is free amine groups in PVAm water solution. Kobayashi et al. [28] also reported that all the amino groups attached to the polymer chain are protonated at pH 3 and pH greatly affects the physicalchemical properties of PVAm. The chemical structure of PVAm at different pH is illustrated in Fig. 2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Furthermore, the overall solubility of CO2 is higher in basic aqueous solutions and a membrane prepared from a casting solution of high pH dissolves more CO2 than a membrane at low pH when the membrane becomes water swollen. The pH of the membrane would therefore be expected to have a large impact on the performance of the PVAm FSC membrane [29]. In this report, PVAm FSC membranes were prepared from casting solutions of different pH and tested in a permeation set-up for CO2 separation from flue gas. The casting solution was first pH-titrated to investigate the shape of the titration curve and to choose suitable pH values for the study. Membranes were then

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The casting solution was titrated by 0.1 M KOH using a Mettler DL25 titrator (Mettler Toledo) equipped with a Mettler Toledo DG 111-SC KCl/AgCl electrode at ambient temperature. The pH was monitored as function of addition of KOH. At the beginning of the experiment the casting solution had a pH of 7. Therefore aqueous HCl was added until a pH of 2.6 was reached, which represents the starting point of the titration.
texts.text: The casting solution was titrated by 0.1 M KOH using a Mettler DL25 titrator (Mettler Toledo) equipped with a Mettler Toledo DG 111-SC KCl/AgCl electrode at ambient temperature. texts.text: The membranes were analyzed by Scanning Electron Microscopy on a FESEM device, Zeiss Ultra 55, Limited Edition using a secondary electron (SE) detector.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PVAm composite membrane was prepared by casting a polyvinylamine (PVAm) solution onto a commercial PSf (polysulfone) ultrafiltration membrane (Alfa Laval AS) according to literature procedures [19-23]. A commercial PVAm (Lupamine TM ) was kindly provided by BASF AG, Germany.
texts.text: The PVAm composite membrane was prepared by casting a polyvinylamine (PVAm) solution onto a commercial PSf (polysulfone) ultrafiltration membrane (Alfa Laval AS) according to literature procedures [19-23]. A commercial PVAm (Lupamine TM ) was kindly provided by BASF AG, Germany.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The commercial PVAm contained about 10% impurities and was therefore not suitable for direct use in membrane preparation. The PVAm was purified in a series of steps: Precipitation in ethanol/acetone, washing, filtration, drying and re-dissolution for casting. The resulting polymer solution was hydrolyzed by aqueous HCl and re-precipitated as the corresponding hydrochloride [21,30]. The polymer was characterized by 1 HNMR. The re-precipitated PVAm HCl was dissolved in water at 0.2 to 1 wt % for casting on a PSf support. Casting solutions of pH 4, 7, 9 and 12 were prepared by controlled addition of dilute aqueous HCl or KOH. Membranes were then prepared according to a standard procedure as follows.
texts.text: The commercial PVAm contained about 10% impurities and was therefore not suitable for direct use in membrane preparation. The PVAm was purified in a series of steps: Precipitation in ethanol/acetone, washing, filtration, drying and re-dissolution for casting. The resulting polymer solution was hydrolyzed by aqueous HCl and re-precipitated as the corresponding hydrochloride [21,30]. The polymer was characterized by 1 HNMR. The re-precipitated PVAm HCl was dissolved in water at 0.2 to 1 wt % for casting on a PSf support. Casting solutions of pH 4, 7, 9 and 12 were prepared by controlled addition of dilute aqueous HCl or KOH. Membranes were then prepared according to a standard procedure as follows.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PSf support (flat sheet type) was cut into a 30 cm -30 cm piece and placed in a steel support. The PSf support was placed over the steel support and a steel frame was placed on top of the membrane. The PSf support fixed by the steel frame was thoroughly washed with distilled water and then dried. The prepared casting solution was then cast over the support to form an even active layer of pre-determined thickness and the membrane was dried overnight. The membrane was then cut into a shape and

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Permeation of the FSC membranes of different pH:s was tested for gas permeation (Fig. 4). The feed gas was supplied from a premixed gas cylinder (10 vol% CO2 in CO2/N2 gas mixture, Yara Praxair AS) at flow rate of about 330 ml/min. A sweep gas was used to achieve better recording of fluxes and gas compositions (CH4, Yara Praxair AS) at flow rate of about 6 ml/min. A sample cut of a flat sheet PVAm membrane (diameter 67 mm) was mounted between the feed and permeate chamber in a small typical lab scale membrane module. Rubber O-rings was used to seal the membrane supported by a porous metal disc. The membrane module with the membrane sample cut was then installed in a thermostatic cabinet equipped with a temperature control system. The feed and sweep gases were humidified with

texts.text: Fig. 3. SEM image of a FSC membrane composed of PVAm top skin layer supported on porous PSf.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: water vapor in all operations. Water humidification of the gases was accomplished by bubbling the gases through a water-filled bottle before entering the membrane module. A bypass line was used to adjust the humidity of the feed gas. Flow and pressure were recorded and controlled by a flow controller, flow indicator and pressure transmitters (MKS). The humidity and temperature data was then directly logged into a computer via a humidity sensor (HW3, Rotronic AG). Composition of the permeate gas was analyzed online by a gas chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector (MicroGC3000, Agilent).
texts.text: water vapor in all operations. Water humidification of the gases was accomplished by bubbling the gases through a water-filled bottle before entering the membrane module. A bypass line was used to adjust the humidity of the feed gas. Flow and pressure were recorded and controlled by a flow controller, flow indicator and pressure transmitters (MKS). The humidity and temperature data was then directly logged into a computer via a humidity sensor (HW3, Rotronic AG). Composition of the permeate gas was analyzed online by a gas chromatograph equipped with a thermal conductivity detector (MicroGC3000, Agilent).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Permeation experiments were carried out at a 1.1 bar and 35 1 C. The relative humidity of the feed gas was controlled in the range of 35-95%. The separation performance (permeance and selectivity) under a certain set of conditions was recorded at least 6-8 times and usually the last 3-4 recordings could be used for a result after the system had been stabilized such that the standard deviation of the last 3-4 recordings became less than 10%. The sample membranes (diameter 67 mm, usually 2-3 cuts from different spots) were cut from the larger membrane of different pH:s prepared in the lab (3.3. Preparation of PVAm/PSf membrane). The difference in membrane performance of these sample cuts from a same pH membrane was almost negligible in most of the cases.
texts.text: Permeation experiments were carried out at a 1.1 bar and 35 1 C. The relative humidity of the feed gas was controlled in the range of 35-95%. The separation performance (permeance and selectivity) under a certain set of conditions was recorded at least 6-8 times and usually the last 3-4 recordings could be used for a result after the system had been stabilized such that the standard deviation of the last 3-4 recordings became less than 10%. The sample membranes (diameter 67 mm, usually 2-3 cuts from different spots) were cut from the larger membrane of different pH:s prepared in the lab (3.3. Preparation of PVAm/PSf membrane). The difference in membrane performance of these sample cuts from a same pH membrane was almost negligible in most of the cases.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purified, partly hydrolyzed PVAm was characterized by 1 H NMR and found to be pure and to contain approximately 10% residual polyvinyl amide. For our application this is considered an advantage because PVAm is a highly crystalline polymer. The residual polyvinyl amide units, randomly distributed along the polymer chain, prevent excessive crystallization of the membrane and increase its free volume. Excessive crystallization of the membrane may cause shrinkage and breakage of the selective layer and should therefore be avoided.
texts.text: The purified, partly hydrolyzed PVAm was characterized by 1 H NMR and found to be pure and to contain approximately 10% residual polyvinyl amide. For our application this is considered an advantage because PVAm is a highly crystalline polymer. The residual polyvinyl amide units, randomly distributed along the polymer chain, prevent excessive crystallization of the membrane and increase its free volume. Excessive crystallization of the membrane may cause shrinkage and breakage of the selective layer and should therefore be avoided.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The curve is quite steep in most of the pH range under study, indicating a high sensitivity of the casting solution to fluctuations in pH; a small addition of base or acid may have a large impact on the pH and the degree of protonation of the solution while it converges at pH 12. A small change in the conditions for preparation of the casting solution, like the addition of slightly different volumes of acid or base each time, may therefore lead to a significant change in the degree of protonation of the membrane unless the pH is carefully monitored.

texts.orig: 4.4. Effect of pH on permeance and selectivity
texts.text: 4.4. Effect of pH on permeance and selectivity

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pH 4 casting solution became markedly more viscous than the other ones due to ionic interactions between the charged PVAm-chains and counter ions in the solution [28,31]. However, membranes could be readily cast from all four solutions. The results of permeance and selectivity of membranes prepared with different pH casting solutions are shown in Figs. 6 and 7 respectively.
texts.text: The pH 4 casting solution became markedly more viscous than the other ones due to ionic interactions between the charged PVAm-chains and counter ions in the solution [28,31]. However, membranes could be readily cast from all four solutions. The results of permeance and selectivity of membranes prepared with different pH casting solutions are shown in Figs. 6 and 7 respectively.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the water swollen PVAm membrane, the water plays a dual role. First, it takes part in the CO2 transport mechanism by reacting with dissolved CO2 to form carbonic acid and subsequently bicarbonate. In this reaction the amine side groups remove a proton from the carbonic acid to form the carbonate

First, it takes part in the CO2 transport mechanism by reacting with dissolved CO2 to form carbonic acid and subsequently bicarbonate. In this reaction the amine side groups remove a proton from the carbonic acid to form the carbonate

texts.label: text
texts.orig: and therefore drive the equilibrium towards the formation of carbonate. Ammonium groups similarly lose a proton to a molecule of bicarbonate to regenerate the amine. The carbonic acid decomposes into water and CO2, which evaporates on the permeate side. The water also acts as a solvent for both the amine carriers and the different CO2 species. At higher relative humidity more water condenses and is adsorbed in the membrane and larger amount of CO2 can therefore be dissolved. The free volume in the selective layer increases due to separation of the polymer chains by water and more volume becomes available for CO2 transport. Therefore the observed increase in permeance and selectivity with increasing relative humidity in the feed gas is expected regardless of the pH of the casting solution as previously reported [19-23].
texts.text: and therefore drive the equilibrium towards the formation of carbonate. Ammonium groups similarly lose a proton to a molecule of bicarbonate to regenerate the amine. The carbonic acid decomposes into water and CO2, which evaporates on the permeate side. The water also acts as a solvent for both the amine carriers and the different CO2 species. At higher relative humidity more water condenses and is adsorbed in the membrane and larger amount of CO2 can therefore be dissolved. The free volume in the selective layer increases due to separation of the polymer chains by water and more volume becomes available for CO2 transport. Therefore the observed increase in permeance and selectivity with increasing relative humidity in the feed gas is expected regardless of the pH of the casting solution as previously reported [19-23].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Both permeance and selectivity increased drastically with increasing pH of the casting solution over the whole humidity range (3595%) as shown in Figs. 6 and 7. At over 90% relative humidity range this trend became more pronounced by a drastic increase in CO2 permeance from 0.2 to 5 m 3 (STP)/m 2 bar h when the pH of the casting solution raised from pH 4 to pH 12. The change of selectivity of CO2 over N2 showed also drastic increase from 90 to 500.
texts.text: Both permeance and selectivity increased drastically with increasing pH of the casting solution over the whole humidity range (3595%) as shown in Figs. 6 and 7. At over 90% relative humidity range this trend became more pronounced by a drastic increase in CO2 permeance from 0.2 to 5 m 3 (STP)/m 2 bar h when the pH of the casting solution raised from pH 4 to pH 12. The change of selectivity of CO2 over N2 showed also drastic increase from 90 to 500.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With increasing pH the number of free amine groups available for reaction with CO2 increases. The increased number of free amine groups increases the possibility for PVAm to react with CO2 under humidified conditions. More amine groups can therefore act as carriers to facilitate the transport of CO2 at higher pH than
texts.text: With increasing pH the number of free amine groups available for reaction with CO2 increases. The increased number of free amine groups increases the possibility for PVAm to react with CO2 under humidified conditions. More amine groups can therefore act as carriers to facilitate the transport of CO2 at higher pH than

texts.label: text
texts.orig: at lower pH where a much larger extent of the amine groups exists in the form of the ammonium salt which is much less reactive towards CO2. Because the aqueous solubility of all species of CO2 is higher at higher pH, the water swollen membranes prepared from pH 12 casting solution is also able to absorb more CO2 than membranes prepared from pH 4 casting solution.
texts.text: at lower pH where a much larger extent of the amine groups exists in the form of the ammonium salt which is much less reactive towards CO2. Because the aqueous solubility of all species of CO2 is higher at higher pH, the water swollen membranes prepared from pH 12 casting solution is also able to absorb more CO2 than membranes prepared from pH 4 casting solution.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The transport of N2 over the membrane on the other hand is not affected by the pH, resulting in the drastically increase in separation performance.
texts.text: The transport of N2 over the membrane on the other hand is not affected by the pH, resulting in the drastically increase in separation performance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Differences in ionic content between membranes prepared at different pH:s may affect the water swelling capacity of the membrane. Increased ionic content might, according to theory, cause the membrane to absorb more water from the humidified gas to shield the ionic charge of the ammonium salt according to the Donnan equilibrium effect [25]. The membrane prepared from pH 4 casting solution would have a higher ionic content than membranes prepared from higher pH casting solutions. Though our swelling tests performed on membrane samples of different pH were non-conclusive and did not verify any such effect, the increased swelling of PVAm at low pH has been already reported by Kobayashi et al. [32]. The higher ionic content would cause the membrane to swell more than the other membranes and therefore the membrane would have more flexible structure and free volume available for non-selective, diffusive transport of CO2 and N2 at low pH.
texts.text: Differences in ionic content between membranes prepared at different pH:s may affect the water swelling capacity of the membrane. Increased ionic content might, according to theory, cause the membrane to absorb more water from the humidified gas to shield the ionic charge of the ammonium salt according to the Donnan equilibrium effect [25]. The membrane prepared from pH 4 casting solution would have a higher ionic content than membranes prepared from higher pH casting solutions. Though our swelling tests performed on membrane samples of different pH were non-conclusive and did not verify any such effect, the increased swelling of PVAm at low pH has been already reported by Kobayashi et al. [32]. The higher ionic content would cause the membrane to swell more than the other membranes and therefore the membrane would have more flexible structure and free volume available for non-selective, diffusive transport of CO2 and N2 at low pH.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All FSC membranes were characterized by Scanning Electron Microscopy, SEM to investigate the surface and thickness of the PVAm selective layer as in Fig. 3. While all membranes cast from neutral to high pH casting solutions were smooth and even, the surface of the pH 4 membrane contained irregularities in a form of small lumps on the membrane surface, as shown in Fig. 8. It is not clear why these irregularities were formed, however we assume it is related with the increased viscosity at lower pH which was markedly observed at the lowest investigated pH 4. This increase of viscosity at pH 4 is most likely due to increased interaction between the charged polymer chains and dissolved ions [32], which complicated smooth surface formation during the membrane preparation. The presence of such surface defects may have an influence on the separation properties of the pH 4 membrane and be a part of the explanation of its relatively lower performance.
texts.text: All FSC membranes were characterized by Scanning Electron Microscopy, SEM to investigate the surface and thickness of the PVAm selective layer as in Fig. 3. While all membranes cast from neutral to high pH casting solutions were smooth and even, the surface of the pH 4 membrane contained irregularities in a form of small lumps on the membrane surface, as shown in Fig. 8. It is not clear why these irregularities were formed, however we assume it is related with the increased viscosity at lower pH which was markedly observed at the lowest investigated pH 4. This increase of viscosity at pH 4 is most likely due to increased interaction between the charged polymer chains and dissolved ions [32], which complicated smooth surface formation during the membrane preparation. The presence of such surface defects may have an influence on the separation properties of the pH 4 membrane and be a part of the explanation of its relatively lower performance.

texts.orig: Polyvinylamine (PVAm) Fixed-Site-Carrier (FSC) membranes have been investigated with respect to their permeation and
texts.text: Polyvinylamine (PVAm) Fixed-Site-Carrier (FSC) membranes have been investigated with respect to their permeation and

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: Fig. 8. SEM image of PVAm FSC membrane (pH 4 casting solution) displaying surface inhomogeneity.
texts.text: Fig. 8. SEM image of PVAm FSC membrane (pH 4 casting solution) displaying surface inhomogeneity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: selectivity as a function of pH. The PVAm casting solution can be pH-titrated and is sensitive towards small changes in the amount of acid or base added during the preparation of the membranes. The separation properties to a large extent were determined by the casting solution pH. The performance both in permeance and selectivity increased drastically when the pH increased from 4 to 12. This is explained by the increased number of free amine groups with increasing pH which are available for facilitation of the transport of CO2. So the pH of the casting solution has a huge impact on the CO2 separation property of PVAm membrane. At low pH, both the decreased number of free amines and the increased viscosity may have resulted in the decreased membrane performance.
texts.text: selectivity as a function of pH. The PVAm casting solution can be pH-titrated and is sensitive towards small changes in the amount of acid or base added during the preparation of the membranes. The separation properties to a large extent were determined by the casting solution pH. The performance both in permeance and selectivity increased drastically when the pH increased from 4 to 12. This is explained by the increased number of free amine groups with increasing pH which are available for facilitation of the transport of CO2. So the pH of the casting solution has a huge impact on the CO2 separation property of PVAm membrane. At low pH, both the decreased number of free amines and the increased viscosity may have resulted in the decreased membrane performance.
